summary: Legacy validated record without machine-checkable validation proof. User policy for storing and showing search history. Good for privacy and a cleaner search pane, but it removes suggestions based on previous searches.

setting.name: Search History Storage and Display
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can remember previous search queries and use them to suggest searches later. This policy decides whether that search history is stored and shown for the current user.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable search history to keep prior queries out of the registry and stop Windows from surfacing suggestions based on earlier searches.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off reduces retained search history and suggestion clutter, but it also removes convenience features based on past searches.
setting.professional_notes: Search.admx maps the user policy to HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer\DisableSearchHistory with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. The local ADML help text says enabling the policy prevents search queries from being stored in the registry and stops previous-search suggestions from appearing.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force the search-history policy for the current user.

allowed_values.label: Search history allowed
allowed_values.meaning: Previous searches can be stored and used for later search suggestions.

allowed_values.label: Search history disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Search queries aren't stored in the registry and previous-search suggestions won't appear in the search pane.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisableSearchHistory = 1, which matches the documented disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows user-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems with the Search policy for DisableSearchHistory available

states.rationale: The user policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: The local ADML help text states that if the policy is disabled or not configured, users get search suggestions based on previous searches.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: People who want search suggestions based on previous queries
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep its normal behavior for search history and previous-search suggestions.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want prior queries kept out of the registry and out of suggestions.
tradeoffs: No policy-based restriction is enforced for stored search history.

recommended_profiles.label: Privacy focused
avoid_for: Users who rely on previous-search suggestions for convenience

states.rationale: Prevent search queries from being stored in the registry and stop suggestions based on previous searches.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want less retained search history and a quieter search pane.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want Windows to recall and suggest your earlier search terms.
tradeoffs: Repeated searches may be slightly less convenient.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft Search.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file shows DisableSearchHistory with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0 under the Explorer policy key.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft Search.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy prevents search queries from being stored in the registry and stops previous-search suggestions from appearing in the search pane.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisableSearchHistory = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak provenance record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: The local Microsoft Search policy files explicitly define the user policy path HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer, the value name DisableSearchHistory, and the enabled/disabled mapping. The app writes the documented enabled-state value 1 that disables stored search history and previous-search suggestions.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Search.admx: <policy name="DisableSearchHistory" class="User" ... key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer" valueName="DisableSearchHistory"> <enabledValue><decimal value="1" /></enabledValue> <disabledValue><decimal value="0" /></disabledValue>; Search.adml: "If you enable this policy setting, search queries will not be stored in the registry and users cannot see or use previous searches in the Search pane."
validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft ADMX file defines the exact user policy path and value name used by the app, with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. The paired ADML help text explains that enabling the policy stops storing search history and removes previous-search suggestions.
